summary: An original modern East-Asian botanical illustration system with bold plant silhouettes, selective ink-like texture, restrained colour blocks, and strong negative-space control, avoiding antique wallpaper symmetry.
visual_vocabulary: controlled pale mist and broken water shapes
modern editorial composition rather than formal antique ornament
material_cues: no baked highlights or glaze reflections
composition_principles: use asymmetry and directional plant growth
separate hero silhouette from supporting foliage
balance dense botanical zones with quiet dark water
keep important forms readable on a narrow weapon body
avoid: text, logos, brands, signatures, watermarks, baked lighting

route_a.objective: Create a dense crop-tolerant botanical template whose repeat is hidden by overlapping organic forms.
composition_rules: cross every edge with partial plants

route_b.objective: Create one weapon-level botanical narrative with an asymmetrical hero and directional plant flow.

component_roles.stock: dark atmospheric opening with cropped supporting foliage

candidate_directions.title: Hero Bloom
candidate_directions.concept: One asymmetrical flower dominates a field of supporting leaves.
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: one receiver bloom controls the weapon hierarchy

candidate_directions.title: Leaf Current
candidate_directions.concept: Broad leaves and directional stems create a strong horizontal current.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: leaf currents interlock across tile edges
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: the current connects stock, receiver, and handguard

candidate_directions.title: Mist and Negative Space
candidate_directions.concept: Sparse botanical silhouettes emerge through broad atmospheric negative space.
candidate_directions.route_a_emphasis: small clusters alternate with quiet gaps
candidate_directions.route_b_emphasis: mist protects the hero and quiet muzzle

reference_policy: Use rights-safe botanical references only for plant anatomy; do not imitate a named living artist or copy an existing illustration.
